channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Quantic Dream Layoffs Hitting Up to 95 Jobs Following Game Closure
item.content:encoded:
Yesterday, Quantic Dream announced that they were closing their long in development 3v3 MOBA-like game Spellcasters Chronicles, after it released in Early access earlier this year. In the Spellcasters Chronicles shutdown announcement there was a hint of Quantic Dream layoffs, but today we have more information about the situation.

French video game employee union STJV (Le Syndicat des Travailleuresuses du Jeu Video) reported today that one quarter of the company's workers are under threat - or 95 different jobs in France. STJV goes on that Spellcasters Chronicles had been in development for 8 years, and lays the flaws of the product at management's feet stating that worker representatives had raised concerns about the risk and high cost of the game numerous times only to be dismissed. According to STJV, management had said that the 30 years of experience in the field by key decision makers meant success was a given, and there was never any consideration or plan for a less successful outcome.

STJV also pins blame on NetEase as the owner of Quantic Dream since 2022. They mention the lack of marketing for the game, which led to few people knowing about it as well as the lack of resources dedicated to it around its early access release. One particular issue that STJV raises is that there were no credits for the game in the early access versions, though it is impossible to download the game to check that right now as it has been removed from 'sale', which also prevents the developers from inviting players to come and see the game one last time, as only those few who already had the game can play it.

STJV Demands Regarding Quantic Dream Layoffs

As part of their letter, STJV issued 4 demands to "safeguard the future of Quantic Dream, its projects, and its workers":

  • Halting all layoffs,
  • Spellcasters team be assigned to work on Star Wars Eclipse or a new project,
  • All employees to be re-catagorized as higher rank based on their work on Spellcasters Chronicles,
  • Management and creative directors resign for their failure.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/quantic-dream-layoffs-hitting-up-to-95-jobs-following-game-closure
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
